name: Oura Ring
short_description: A finger-worn smart ring that continuously tracks sleep, heart rate, body temperature, and activity, surfacing daily readiness and health insights through an app.
long_description: The Oura Ring is a titanium smart ring packed with optical heart-rate, temperature, and motion sensors that quietly logs physiological signals all day and night from the finger. Software turns this raw biometric stream into interpreted scores for sleep, readiness, and activity, plus longer-term trends and anomaly alerts. As a piece of always-worn jewelry rather than a screened gadget, it shows how ambient, sensor-driven AI can read the body intimately and continuously, framing health as an ongoing background computation woven into daily life.

images.caption: An Oura smart ring on display at an electronics retailer.
images.alt_text: A dark titanium Oura smart ring shown in a retail display, with the band's inner sensors and finish visible.

sources.title: Oura Ring. Smart Ring for Fitness, Stress, Sleep & Health.
Oura Health
sources.access_notes: Company and product history: founded 2013 in Oulu, Finland; first ring via Kickstarter in 2015-2016; Gen 2 (2018), Gen 3 (2021), Ring 4 (2024).

interactions.title: The ring notices you're getting sick first
interactions.description: Days before you feel run-down, Symptom Radar flags that your skin temperature, resting heart rate, and respiratory rate are drifting off baseline and quietly suggests you might be coming down with something.

interactions.title: Read your cycle from body temperature
interactions.description: Without logging anything, the ring tracks the temperature shift around ovulation to map your fertile window and predict periods months out, accurate enough to detect ovulation in over 96% of cycles in a validation study.

interactions.title: Ask your own body data a question
interactions.description: Type "why was my sleep bad this week?" into Oura Advisor and an LLM-powered companion answers using your own short- and long-term metrics, drawing charts and suggesting a plan rather than handing you raw numbers.

interactions.title: An early-warning system for a whole league
interactions.description: During the 2020 NBA bubble, more than 2,000 players and staff wore Oura rings whose temperature and heart-rate signals fed a daily illness-risk score, part of a protocol that finished the season with zero player COVID cases.

interactions.title: It knew she was pregnant before the test did
interactions.description: Wearers describe noticing a sustained rise in nightly temperature and resting heart rate weeks before a positive test or a missed period, reading conception in the data before they consciously suspected it.

interactions.title: Replay last night's drink
interactions.description: Wake up to see a dented readiness score and a flattened heart-rate-variability curve, and read directly off the graph how a late dinner or a couple of glasses of wine cost you recovery — grounded in the HRV, resting-heart-rate, and temperature streams the ring already logs overnight.

interactions.title: Hand the trends to your clinician
interactions.description: Bring months of continuous temperature, heart-rate, and respiratory baselines to an appointment so a doctor can see the slow drift behind a symptom — using the long-term biometric history the ring continuously stores rather than a single in-office snapshot.
